source: Learning Ansible with Rocky - Large Scale Infrastructure: Ansible tags

questions.q: What do Ansible tags let you do?
options: Run or skip a subset of the tasks in your code
questions.why: Tags let you execute or skip part of the tasks.

questions.q: How do you attach a tag to a task?
options: Add a tags: key to the task (e.g. tags: users)
questions.why: A tags: key on the task (or role) names its tag(s).

questions.q: Which ansible-playbook option runs ONLY the tasks with a given tag?
questions.why: --tags <name> limits the run to tasks carrying that tag.

questions.q: Which option does the opposite -- skips tasks with a given tag?
questions.why: --skip-tags <name> runs everything except the tagged tasks.

options: Both individual tasks and whole roles
questions.why: The chapter tags both tasks and entire roles.
